records.ety: [OF. rebouquier to dull, blunt, F. reboucher; perhaps fr. pref. re- re- + bouche mouth, OF. also bouque, L. bucca cheek; if so, the original sense was, to stop the mouth of; hence, to stop, obstruct.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Rebuked (r*būkt"); p. pr. & vb. n. Rebuking.]

defs: To check, silence, or put down, with reproof; to restrain by expression of disapprobation; to reprehend sharply and summarily; to chide; to reprove; to admonish.
quotes: The proud he tamed, the penitent he cheered, Nor to rebuke the rich offender feared.

defs: A direct and pointed reproof; a reprimand; also, chastisement; punishment.
quotes: For thy sake I have suffered rebuke.
Why bear you these rebukes and answer not?
